description: Inside a brightly lit nail salon, Rachel and Chandler sit in white rounded chairs, receiving manicures from two technicians. Another technician gives a pedicure to an unidentified person in the foreground.
Chandler looks at his left hand, extending his fingers, then turns to Rachel and says, "I can't believe I'm getting my nails done. And you said it was going to be fun."
Chandler closes his eyes briefly, raising his left hand slightly, and says, "Which it kind of is."
"Also, you said there were going to be other guys here. There are no other guys here," Chandler says to Rachel, gesturing with his right hand toward the room.

Pointing with a nail file over her shoulder, Rachel says, "Chandler. There's a guy right over there." Chandler looks toward a man in a postal uniform talking to a woman at the counter and replies, "That's a mailman."
"That's our mailman," Chandler says, continuing to look toward the background.
Chandler raises his hand in a brief wave and says, "Hi."
Rachel waves slightly toward the background and says, "How are you?"
The mailman waves back and walks out of the salon. Chandler watches him leave, then looks down at his hand.
"Chandler, don't worry. This doesn't make you any less of a guy," Rachel says, looking at Chandler while he keeps his gaze forward.
"That does," Chandler says, raising his right hand to display his freshly manicured nails.
Chandler lowers his hand and shifts his weight in the chair, his eyebrows drawing together slightly.
"What am I sitting on?" Chandler asks, reaching behind himself to pull a small red object from the chair.
Chandler holds the red object, turning it slightly in his hand to examine it.
"I'd hate to think what this woman was scratching when this broke off," Chandler says to Rachel, holding the object up. Rachel looks at the object and wrinkles her nose.
"Hey, you know who used to have nails like that? Oh, my God," Chandler asks Rachel. Janice abruptly appears in the doorway, waving her hands rapidly and opening her mouth wide.
Chandler stares wide-eyed at Janice in silence, while Rachel turns her head to look.
The setting changes to the exterior of a multi-story brick apartment building under dim lighting.

The scene changes to the interior of an apartment. Monica stands by the front door, flipping a light switch up and down, while Joey leans on the kitchen counter and Rachel stands nearby holding a bottle.
"Joey, let me ask you a question. This has been driving me crazy. What does this light switch do?" Monica asks, flipping the switch again and turning to Joey.
"Nothing," Joey answers, shrugging slightly.
"Did it drive you crazy to have a switch and not know what it did?" Monica asks, stepping away from the wall. "I know what it did," Joey responds from his position at the counter.
"Nothing," Joey adds.
"They wouldn't have put it there if it didn't do something," Monica says, raising her hands to emphasize her words.
"How can you not care?" Monica asks Joey.
"Like this," Joey says, shrugging with his hands spread wide.
Monica turns away from Joey and Rachel, walks back to the wall, and flips the light switch again.

Rachel sets her bottle on the counter and asks, "Well, here's another question for you. Do you know what that little silver knob on the toilet does?" Joey gestures toward her and answers, "Sure. It flushes it." Rachel nods, adding, "Okay, good."
"Now, since you know, when you come over, would you mind actually using it?" Rachel asks, pointing at Joey. Joey raises his hands slightly.
Joey remains quiet, his hands still raised. Chandler opens the front door and steps into the apartment.
"Hello?" Chandler calls out as he walks in.
"Hey," Joey greets him. Janice follows Chandler inside, shouting, "Stop," loudly.
Janice laughs and approaches Monica, who looks surprised.
"Oh, my God," Monica says, smiling.
"Guess what we ran into today," Chandler says to the room, gesturing toward Janice.
Joey points at Janice with both hands and takes a step toward her.
"Goodness, is this amazing?" Janice says, pulling Monica into a hug and laughing.
"How have you been?" Monica asks. "Well, I'm divorced," Janice answers, clasping her hands together. Phoebe enters the kitchen area in the background.
"Wow," Joey and Phoebe say together. "Yeah, I'm riding the alimony pony," Janice says, laughing loudly and bending forward.
Janice throws her head back, continuing her loud, nasal laugh. Phoebe smiles as she stands next to Joey.
"And there it is," Phoebe says, holding a magazine and looking at Janice.
"I just came up to say hi," Janice says, standing near the door.
"Hi," Monica, Joey, and Rachel say in unison.
"And you, sweetie. I'll see you later," Janice says, touching Chandler's shoulder and kissing his cheek. "Okay. I'll see you tonight," Chandler replies. "Okay. Bye," Janice adds.
"Bye," Janice says, pulling the door open.
Janice steps out into the hallway and pulls the door closed behind her.
"Bye," several people say simultaneously.
"I can't stand the woman," Chandler says, standing by the door with a flat expression.
Phoebe and Joey look at Chandler, their eyes widening in surprise.

"What? I thought you were crazy about her," Phoebe asks. "I know, but you know all those little annoying things that she did before we fell in love? Like," Chandler responds, taking a step forward and gesturing with his hands.
"her voice and her laugh and her personality? Well, they're all back," Chandler continues, raising his hands, his vocal pitch rising as he lists the items.
Chandler briefly pauses, lowering his hands to rest near his hips.
"and she's picked up, like, nine new ones," Chandler continues, gesturing. Joey points forcefully at the floor and asks, "So what are you doing bringing her here? There's people here."
Chandler steps further into the living area, gesturing with one hand toward the kitchen.

"Don't worry about it. I'm taking care of it tonight," Chandler says to Joey. Monica gestures at Chandler from the kitchen, saying, "You are not. You have never been able to break up with her." Chandler replies, "Well,"
"I don't have to break up with her this time. We're not involved. I'm going to do a preemptive strike," Chandler says, pulling a bottle from the refrigerator and speaking while holding it.
"I'm going to end it with her before it starts," Chandler says, emphasizing his words with a firm, downward hand gesture.
"My ass is, like, frozen," Chandler says after a slight pause, gesturing behind himself.
Chandler sets his bottle on the counter. Joey leans forward slightly, looking intently at him.
"Try sticking it in the freezer for 20 minutes," Joey says, crossing his arms over his chest.
Chandler stares at Joey with a flat expression as Joey holds his crossed-arm posture.
In the kitchen, Joey, Chandler, Rachel, Phoebe, and Monica stand together. Chandler says, "I'm telling you."
The view changes to the illuminated New York City skyline at night.
Now on a dimly lit city street at night, Ross and Emily walk side by side. Ross holds a white cup, and Emily wears a dark, fur-collared coat.
Emily says, "I can't believe you really walk alone here at night. I mean, you hear such stories about New York." Ross turns his head toward her and replies, "No, it's really not that bad."
Ross gestures with his free hand and says, "I mean, I, for one, feel perfectly safe." Audience laughter is audible.
Three men in athletic wear suddenly run into the frame, tackling each other to the ground beside Ross and Emily. Ross recoils and shouts, "Help! Help!"
The men stand up. Emily smiles, stepping toward one of the men. Ross watches while clutching his cup, as audience laughter continues.
Emily gestures between the men and Ross. Ross steps forward, patting one man's shoulder while holding his cup, and laughs.
Ross points toward the spot where the men fell, smiling and talking to them.
Emily speaks to Liam, saying, "I've been meaning to ring you ever since I arrived, but, well, I've been rather busy. Do you realize we have not"
Emily finishes, "seen each other since the night that U2 concert?" Liam replies, "Oh, my God. I think you're right."
Liam steps closer to Emily and says, "Well, actually, the last time you and I saw each other was the next morning."
Emily lowers her gaze, smiling.
Emily tilts her head slightly and says, "Oh, Liam."
Ross puts his arm around Emily's shoulders.
Ross laughs loudly and says, "Oh, Liam."
Ross stops laughing abruptly and drops his arm.
Ross looks at Liam and asks, "So, what, were you guys playing soccer or something? Or should I call it football?"
Liam looks at Ross.
Liam holds a beverage can and says, "We were playing rugby. In fact, we're playing a game at the park tomorrow."
Liam points his can at Ross and adds, "You're welcome to play too, if you want."
Emily turns to Ross and says, "Ross? Play rugby?"
Emily laughs briefly and says, "I don't think so."
Ross's smile fades as he stares at Emily. Audience laughter is audible.
Ross asks, "What's so funny about that?"
Emily replies, "Well, I mean, you're American, to start with. You don't really have rugby here." Ross begins, "Well,"
Ross gestures with his cup and says, "we didn't have freedom here until 1776 either, so good."
Liam gestures with his can and says, "Then we'll see you at Riverside Park at two."
Liam raises his can, and Ross raises his cup. They both say, "Cheers."
Liam drinks from his can. Ross brings his cup to his mouth, but his grip crushes the cup, spilling hot coffee onto his hand. The cup crunches audibly amid audience laughter.
Ross recoils, exclaiming, "Ah," while shaking his hand.
Ross wipes his hand on his jacket while Emily looks at him. The scene changes to a neon sign shaped like a coffee cup glowing against a brick wall.
Inside the coffee shop, Janice walks in wearing a white fur coat, followed by Chandler. She says loudly, "boy, I just love to sing."
Chandler stands near the counter, gesturing with his hands, and says, "Yes, I know that you do. I think one of the reasons people were complaining, though was because most of the people"
Chandler continues, "had paid to hear the actor sing Old Man River."
Janice sits on the sofa, pulling off her coat.
Janice smiles and says, "Oh, look at us. Who would have thought that Cupid had a station at 14th Street Nails?"
Janice leans forward and laughs loudly, her mouth wide open. Audience laughter plays.
Chandler steps closer to the sofa, his expression serious, and says, "Okay, we have to talk."
Chandler stands over Janice.
Chandler says, "I'm just getting out of a very serious relationship." Janice nods, replying, "I know."
Janice raises her hands and says, "And I'm just getting out of a marriage. I mean,"
Janice smiles broadly, continuing, "talk about meant to be,"
Janice keeps her hands raised and maintains her smile.
Janice says, "right?"
Chandler holds his hands out and says, "But I just think this is happening too soon." Janice replies, "Oh, too soon to schmoon?"
Janice reaches out and touches Chandler's arm.
Janice says, "Fate. Honey, I am not letting you get away this time."
Chandler looks at her with his mouth slightly open.
Chandler nods slowly and says, "I hear you."
Chandler turns away from Janice, exhales audibly, and paces around the back of the sofa. Audience laughter plays.
Chandler stops pacing, turns to Janice, points a finger upward, and says, "But unfortunately my company is transferring me overseas."
Janice's smile drops as she looks up at Chandler.
Janice says, "Oh, no."
Chandler looks toward her.
Janice asks, "Where to?"
Audience laughter plays as Chandler looks down, lowering his gaze.
Janice asks, "Paris?"
Chandler shakes his head.
Chandler says, "No, not Paris." Janice points a finger and asks, "To London?"
Janice asks, "Rome?"
Janice rapidly says, "Vienna? Oh, Barcelona." Chandler raises his hands to his temples and says, "Okay, could you just stop talking for a second?"
Chandler rubs his temples, his eyes closed. He then lowers his hands.
Chandler says, "Yemen."
Janice gasps audibly and brings her hands to her face. Chandler quickly stands up and steps away from the sofa.
Chandler faces Janice and says loudly, "That's right. Yes, I'm being transferred to Yemen."
Janice looks up at Chandler, lowering her hands, and asks, "When?"
Chandler looks away and says, "I don't know exactly."
Janice sighs, drops her shoulders, and looks down.
Janice stands, steps close to Chandler, places her hands on his chest, and says, "Well, I will just have to soak up every ounce of Chandler Bing until that moment..."
"...comes," Janice finishes.
Chandler looks down at Janice, his posture remaining rigid.
Chandler says, "But I do know it's sometime tomorrow."
The scene changes to a city street at night, with a bus driving past. The setting then changes to an apartment kitchen. Monica stands by the counter and unplugs a coffee maker.
In the adjacent living room area, Phoebe kneels by an end table. Monica asks, "Are you done?"
Phoebe replies, "Yep," keeping one hand flat against the furniture.
Rachel and Joey walk into the apartment.
Rachel asks, "Hey, what's up?" Monica turns to them and says, "This switch thing has been driving me crazy."
Monica gestures toward the wall as she says, "So I turned it off and checked every outlet."
Monica holds up her hands, saying, "Now, four of them don't work, which means one of them has to be controlled by the switch." Joey stands listening, his arms at his sides.
Monica lowers her hands.
Monica gestures widely, saying, "So I plugged in things in all four outlets that make noise. That way, when I turn it on, I just follow the noise and find..."
"...out which one it is," Monica concludes.
Rachel stands with her hands on her hips, looking at Monica.
Joey says, "I better stop listening for. You did."
Rachel shifts her posture.
Rachel points at Monica and asks, "You also could have used lamps and then followed the lights?" Monica turns and walks toward the front door.
Monica arrives at the front door.
Monica stops, gestures toward herself, and says, "Yeah, well, I'm using noise."
Monica places her hand on a wall switch.
Monica asks, "Okay?"
Rachel and Joey watch her from the kitchen area.
Monica rubs her hands together, asks, "All right, so everybody ready?", and then returns her hand to the switch, saying, "Here we go."
Monica flips the switch. A faint humming noise begins as audience laughter plays. Monica slowly steps toward the living room area.
Monica gestures around the room and says, "I hear something. Where is it..."
The humming noise continues. Monica and Rachel walk slowly around the room, looking around. Joey stands still with his hands on his hips.
Joey continues to stand stiffly, shifting his head slightly while the humming noise persists.
Rachel stops walking and looks toward Joey.
"...coming from?" Monica continues.
Rachel stares at Joey and says, "Joey."
Phoebe, still kneeling on the sofa, turns to look at Joey.
Phoebe points at him and says, "Oh, my God."
Phoebe continues pointing at Joey and says loudly, "That's so freaky. Turn him off."
Joey remains frozen in place, his eyes wide.
The scene changes to Joey, Ross, and Rachel sitting in the apartment living room watching a television. A sports commentator's voice says, "Goes back to England with the sack scrub in Sack Field."
The rugby match plays on the television screen.
Phoebe enters wearing a dark coat and says, "Oh, hey. Hi. Could we put on the news? I think it might be raining."
Ross turns his head away from the television to look at Phoebe.
Ross says, "Just hold on a SEC."
Ross gestures toward the television and says, "I'm watching this rugby thing on ESPN. I don't know what the big deal is. I'm man enough to play this sport."
Joey looks at Ross and says, "Dude, you're not even man enough to order the channel that carries the sport."
Ross stares silently at Joey.
Chandler walks into the apartment carrying folded cardboard boxes, followed closely by Janice. Janice says, "Hey there, Ross."
Ross looks over his shoulder at them.
Ross says, "Hey, Janice."
Chandler walks past the others with the boxes.
Chandler stops, turns to Janice, and says, "You know, you really didn't have to help me pack." Janice replies, "Oh?"
Janice raises a large roll of bubble wrap.
Holding the bubble wrap over her shoulder, Janice says, "Well, when you said all you were going to be doing between now and the time you leave is packing you really didn't leave me much choice,"
"...did you?" Janice finishes. Chandler replies, "Well, I thought I did, but I guess I did not."
Joey stands up from his chair and walks toward Chandler.
Joey gestures and asks, "Hey, what's going?"
Chandler turns to face Joey.
Chandler points at himself and says, "Packing. You know, I'm packing because I'm moving to Yemen tomorrow."
Joey looks at Chandler, his mouth slightly open.
Joey gestures with his hands and says loudly, "Thanks for telling me."
Janice forcefully places the bubble wrap onto a box. Chandler quickly steps closer to Joey.
Chandler leans in and speaks softly, "I'm going to pretend I'm moving to Yemen, okay?"
Chandler adds, "It's the only way I can get rid of her." Joey smiles, raises his thumb, and says loudly, "Good one."
Joey continues to smile at Chandler.
Joey nods and asks, "And Yemen?"
Joey adds, "That actually sounds like a real country." Chandler nods tightly.
Chandler keeps his lips pressed together while looking at Joey.
In an apartment living room, Phoebe sits in a brown leather recliner. Joey, Rachel, and Ross stand nearby.

Janice says from off-screen, "Come on, I'm going to show you how to roll up your underwear and stuff it in your shoes. This is a real space saver." Chandler walks into the room, his jaw tight. Phoebe says, "Yeah, no, I"
Phoebe gestures lightly with her hand as she finishes, "do that because it makes me look taller,"
Chandler stands quietly, looking toward the hallway with his lips pressed together.
From the hallway, Janice asks, "okay?"

Janice leans her head around the doorframe and says, "Chandler, come on." Chandler replies, "Okay." He turns to Joey and asks, "Joey, trade lives with me." Joey, standing with his arms crossed, says, "Nope."
Joey shakes his head slightly, keeping his arms crossed. Chandler walks away toward the hallway, and Ross sits on a coffee table in front of the television.

Joey points at the television showing a rugby match. "Man, look at this. Ross, I can't believe you said you'd play rugby," he says. "I mean, look how brutal this is." Ross turns his head from the screen and says, "Hey, I can handle"
Ross completes his sentence, "it, all right?" Rachel, standing nearby with a green bottle in hand, says, "Please."
Rachel looks at Ross and says, "Ross, you got hurt playing badminton with my dad."
Ross turns his gaze back toward Rachel.
Ross stares at Rachel and says, "That's because your mom's dog kept looking at me."
Rachel stares silently at Ross, while Joey steps closer to the television.
Joey points at the rugby players on the screen. "Okay, Ross, look, right here. It's called a scrum, okay?" he says.
Joey continues pointing at the screen and adds, "It's kind of like a huddle."
Ross looks up at Joey and says, "And it is a kind of like a scrutle."
Joey leans over, resting his hands on his thighs as he looks directly at Ross.
Joey leans closer and says, "Ross, they're going to kill you."
Ross looks away toward the floor. Phoebe sits back in the recliner behind them.
Phoebe asks, "Why are you doing this? Anyway?" Ross stands up from the table.
Ross gestures with his hands open and says, "You should have seen the guy that you used to go out with. I mean, he's like Joe Rugby."
Phoebe leans her head back slightly and says, "You're kidding? And he plays rugby? That's so funny."
Ross stands with his hands in his pockets, looking at Phoebe without speaking.
Phoebe waves her hand dismissively and says, "Oh, I see. You did that. All right."
Ross lowers his head briefly before looking back up at her.
Ross gestures outward with his hands and says, "Anyway, she thought the very idea of me playing rugby with him was, like, hilarious."
Ross points a finger toward himself, stating, "So I'm going to show her how tough I really am."
Joey and Rachel stand quietly. Rachel turns her attention fully to Ross, taking a small step forward.
Rachel places a hand on her chest and says, "I'm sorry. I'm sorry. You're right. You are a tough guy."
Rachel adds, "You are the toughest paleontologist I know."
Joey steps next to Ross, places a hand on his shoulder, and says, "All right, look, Ross could take care of himself."
Joey continues, "It's not like he's Chandler." Ross nods and replies, "Thanks."
Ross turns away from Joey, gesturing with both hands.
Ross says, "Look, don't worry about me, okay? I'll just look real energetic and stay away from the ball."
Ross points at the television and says, "I'll be that guy right outside the circle."
On the television screen, a player standing outside a huddle is aggressively tackled to the ground by another player.
Ross, Rachel, and Joey all flinch backward. Ross says, "Oh," while staring at the screen.
Ross stands up straight, placing his hands near his hips, his eyes remaining wide.
Rachel looks at Ross and says, "well, maybe there was a dog looking at him."
Joey pats Ross's back. The scene changes to a sunny park field where many people are gathered on the grass.
Men in colorful rugby uniforms warm up on the field, with some forming a tight huddle.
Ross, wearing a blue and yellow rugby shirt and knee pads, walks toward the playing area holding a rugby ball, followed by Joey, Phoebe, and a woman in a black coat.
Joey points toward the field and says, "Ross. Ross. Ross."
Joey continues to point out different players. "Ross. Stay away from that guy and that guy. And that one. Dude, they're all huge," he says.
Ross holds the rugby ball with both hands, his facial muscles tense as he looks at the opposing team.
Ross stares forward without moving, while Joey beside him continues to observe the players on the field.
